playbook.intent: Empty inbox → 10 personalised cold DMs sent and logged with status by EOD.
playbook.scope: One sitting per week: 10 personalised cold DMs sent on X or LinkedIn to ICP-fit accounts, all logged with status in a minimal CRM so the next batch can compound on prior outcomes.

success-criteria.criterion: Each DM logged with status (sent / replied / booked)
≥1 conversation moved to call or offer

stage.name: Build List
stage.intent: 10 ICP-fit accounts with a personal hook on each.

tasks.task: Note one personal hook per account (recent post, mutual interest)

stage.decision-gate: Advance only once each row has a personal hook.

stage.name: Draft

tasks.task: Open with the personal hook, not your pitch
State a clear ask within 3 lines
Pass the 'would they reply?' bar before sending

stage.decision-gate: Advance only after a peer or fresh eye signs off.

stage.name: Send
stage.intent: All 10 DMs sent inside a focus block.

tasks.task: Send in batches of 5 across two channels max
Log each in the mini-CRM with timestamp

stage.decision-gate: Done once all 10 are sent and logged.

extra.value: [Mass-send tooling or scraped lists, Auto-DM bots — every message is personal, Long sales cycles — short feedback loops only]
[An ICP definition with 50 candidate accounts, A working X or LinkedIn account in good standing]
[Treating the playbook as a checklist instead of a decision tree, Skipping decision gates when intermediate progress feels good, Letting tools or platforms replace the founder's own judgement]

['Did each stage produce its named output or get explicitly skipped with a note?', 'Were decision gates respected or quietly bypassed?', 'Is the evidence trail strong enough to defend the verdict externally?']

{human: faion get-content cold-dm-outreach-batch-10-prospects --format md, agent: faion get-content cold-dm-outreach-batch-10-prospects --format context, json: faion get-content cold-dm-outreach-batch-10-prospects --format json}
[{version: 0.1.0, date: 2026-05-20, note: 'Initial draft from brainstorm-2026-05-17, persona P2 (indie hacker)'}]
